metadata.title:
OPPORTUNITY


TALKS SCHOOL

QUESTION OF CONSOLIDATION
OR A NEW DISTRICT


THRESHED OUT,

pages.raw_text:
The Orchard Park and Cpportun-
ity residents who are putting up a
strong fight for consolidation or a
separate school district, are to have
consolidation, if possible, and if not
new district will be formed


A lively session was held in
Judge Huneke's courtroom this
morning, over fifty residents from
the affected district attending the
meeting, called by Superintendent
of Schools Mcfarland to discuss
the matter.


a. r. Moore of Dishman conduc.
ed the fight for the 81 residents
who are after consolidation or n
new district, and stated this morn
ing that a new district is assured
in the event that the two districts
will not consolidate.


The reason for the agitation for
a new district Is that the IA schol
ars represented by the petitioners
are compelled to walk as far as 21a
miles to attend school.
If the consolidation proposition
passes when put to a vote, a new
schoolhouse will be built, while the
formation of a new district will re-
suit in the same action.
